task_completion.description: Open the Stock Indicator Scanner in JStock
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JStock application is open and the 'Stock Indicator Scanner' tab or window is active.

task_completion.description: Select Relative Strength Index (14) as the indicator
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the rule configuration area, 'Relative Strength Index (14)' or 'RSI' is selected as the indicator.

task_completion.description: Set the operator to Less Than
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the rule configuration area, the operator is set to 'Less Than' or '<'.
In the rule configuration area, the value '30' is entered in the threshold field.

task_completion.description: Save the strategy with the exact name 'Oversold RSI'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The strategy is saved and the name 'Oversold RSI' is visible in a save dialog, strategy list, or as the active strategy name.

integrity.description: The agent used the JStock GUI to create the strategy
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the JStock application interface, not just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to create the configuration files
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) open editing JStock configuration files directly.

integrity.description: The strategy creation process appears genuine
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual progression of opening the scanner, configuring the rule, and saving it within JStock.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The strategy must be named exactly 'Oversold RSI'. The rule must use the indicator 'Relative Strength Index (14)' (or 'RSI'), the operator 'Less Than' (or '<'), and the value '30'. This must be configured via the JStock GUI.
